frontmatter.title: Channels Configuration
frontmatter.description: Configure CLI, Telegram, and Feishu channels

html:
Channels Configuration

LoongClaw supports multiple communication channels: CLI, Telegram, and Feishu/Lark.

CLI Channel

The CLI channel is the default command-line interface.

[cli]
enabled = true
system_prompt = "You are LoongClaw, a helpful assistant."
exit_commands = ["exit", "quit", "q"]
Option Type Default Description
enabled bool true Enable CLI channel
system_prompt string Default prompt System prompt for the AI
exit_commands array ["exit", "quit", "q"] Commands to exit chat
personality string - Prompt personality preset
system_prompt_addendum string - Additional system prompt

Custom System Prompt

[cli]
system_prompt = """
You are LoongClaw, a professional coding assistant.

- Proficient in Rust, Python, JavaScript
- Provide concise, accurate code examples
- Explain technical concepts clearly
"""

Telegram Channel

Enable Telegram bot integration for remote access.

Configuration

[telegram]
enabled = true
bot_token_env = "TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN"
base_url = "https://api.telegram.org"
polling_timeout_s = 30
allowed_chat_ids = []
Option Type Default Description
enabled bool false Enable Telegram channel
bot_token_env string "TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN" Bot token env variable
bot_token string - Bot token (inline, not recommended)
base_url string "https://api.telegram.org" Telegram API URL
polling_timeout_s number 30 Polling timeout in seconds
allowed_chat_ids array [] Allowed chat IDs (empty = all)

Multiple Telegram Accounts

[telegram]
enabled = true
default_account = "main"

[telegram.accounts.main]
bot_token_env = "TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN_MAIN"

[telegram.accounts.backup]
bot_token_env = "TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN_BACKUP"

Security Best Practices

  1. Store token in environment variable:

    export TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN="123456789:ABCdefGHIjklMNOpqrsTUVwxyz"

  2. Restrict allowed chats:

    [telegram]
allowed_chat_ids = [123456789, 987654321]

  3. Use HTTPS proxy if needed:

    HTTPS_PROXY=http://proxy.example.com:8080 loongclaw telegram-serve

Setup Steps

  1. Create a bot via @BotFather
  2. Copy the bot token
  3. Set environment variable: export TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN="your-token"
  4. Add configuration to config.toml
  5. Run: loongclaw telegram-serve

Feishu/Lark Channel

Enable Feishu or Lark integration for enterprise environments.

Configuration

[feishu]
enabled = true
domain = "feishu"                    # "feishu" or "lark"
app_id_env = "FEISHU_APP_ID"
app_secret_env = "FEISHU_APP_SECRET"
verification_token_env = "FEISHU_VERIFICATION_TOKEN"
encrypt_key_env = "FEISHU_ENCRYPT_KEY"
base_url = "https://open.feishu.cn"
webhook_bind = "0.0.0.0:3000"
webhook_path = "/webhook/feishu"
receive_id_type = "chat_id"
allowed_chat_ids = []
ignore_bot_messages = true
Option Type Default Description
enabled bool false Enable Feishu channel
domain string "feishu" Domain: feishu or lark
app_id_env string "FEISHU_APP_ID" App ID env variable
app_secret_env string "FEISHU_APP_SECRET" App secret env variable
verification_token_env string - Verification token env variable
encrypt_key_env string - Encrypt key env variable
base_url string "https://open.feishu.cn" Feishu API URL
webhook_bind string - Webhook bind address
webhook_path string - Webhook path
receive_id_type string "chat_id" ID type: chat_id, open_id, union_id, user_id
allowed_chat_ids array [] Allowed chat IDs
ignore_bot_messages bool true Ignore bot messages

Environment Variables

export FEISHU_APP_ID="cli_xxxxxx"
export FEISHU_APP_SECRET="your-app-secret"
export FEISHU_VERIFICATION_TOKEN="your-verification-token"
export FEISHU_ENCRYPT_KEY="your-encrypt-key"  # Optional

Lark Configuration

For Lark (international version), set domain = "lark":

[feishu]
enabled = true
domain = "lark"
base_url = "https://open.larksuite.com"
# ... other options

Feishu Integration Settings

[feishu_integration]
sqlite_path = "~/.loongclaw/feishu.sqlite3"
oauth_state_ttl_s = 600
request_timeout_s = 20
retry_max_attempts = 4

Setup Steps

  1. Create Feishu Application

    • Log in to Feishu Open Platform
    • Create an enterprise self-built application
    • Record App ID and App Secret

  2. Configure Permissions

    • im:message:send
    • im:message:receive
    • im:chat:readonly

  3. Configure Event Subscription

    • Enable "Receive Messages" event
    • Set webhook URL: http://your-server:3000/webhook/feishu
    • Record Verification Token and Encrypt Key

  4. Start the Service

    loongclaw feishu-serve

Multiple Channels

Enable multiple channels simultaneously:

[cli]
enabled = true

[telegram]
enabled = true
bot_token_env = "TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN"

[feishu]
enabled = true
app_id_env = "FEISHU_APP_ID"

Running Multiple Services

Each channel runs independently:

# Terminal 1: CLI
loongclaw chat

# Terminal 2: Telegram
loongclaw telegram-serve

# Terminal 3: Feishu
loongclaw feishu-serve

Channel Comparison

Feature CLI Telegram Feishu
Setup Difficulty Simple Medium Complex
Network Local Internet Internet
Use Case Development Personal/Team Enterprise
Group Support No Yes Yes
Rich Messages No Markdown Cards

ACP Integration

Enable Agent Control Plane for channels:

[telegram]
enabled = true
bot_token_env = "TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN"

[telegram.acp]
bootstrap_mcp_servers = []
working_directory = "/home/user/workspace"

[feishu]
enabled = true
# ... other config

[feishu.acp]
bootstrap_mcp_servers = []
working_directory = "/home/user/workspace"
